9	import java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy;
10	import java.lang.annotation.Target;

12	/**
13	 * Configure the Kubernetes API to which we want to create a deep link
14	 */
15	@Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME)
16	@Target({ElementType.METHOD, ElementType.FIELD})
17	public @interface KubeLink {
18	    /**
19	     * Gets the API group to which the link should point.
20	     *
21	     * @return  API group to which the link should point
22	     */
23	    String group();
24	
25	    /**
26	     * Gets the API version to which the link should point.
27	     *
28	     * @return  APi version to which the link should point
29	     */
30	    String version();
31	
32	    /**
33	     * Gets the kind to which the link should point.
34	     *
35	     * @return  Kind to which the link should point
36	     */
37	    String kind();
38	}

5	package io.strimzi.crdgenerator;
6	
7	import io.strimzi.crdgenerator.annotations.KubeLink;
8	
9	/**
10	 * CLass for handling links to Kubernetes documentation
11	 */
12	public class KubeLinker implements Linker {
13	    private final String baseUrl;
14	
15	    /**
16	     * Constructs the KubeLinker and initializes the base URL to the Kubernetes documentation
17	     *
18	     * @param baseUrl   The base URL to the Kubernetes documentation which should be used
19	     */
20	    public KubeLinker(String baseUrl) {
21	        // E.g. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.11/
22	        this.baseUrl = baseUrl;
23	    }
24	
25	    /**
26	     * Generates an URL to the Kubernetes documentation to some specific part of the Kube API
27	     *
28	     * @param kubeLink  Specifies the Kubernetes API which should the link point to
29	     *
30	     * @return  An HTTP link deep-linking to the Kubernetes documentation
31	     */
32	    @Override
33	    public String link(KubeLink kubeLink) {
34	        // E.g. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.11/#networkpolicyingressrule-v1-networking-k8s-io
35	        return baseUrl + "#" + kubeLink.kind() + "-" + kubeLink.version() + "-" + kubeLink.group().replace(".", "-");
36	    }
37	}
38

content.text:
Now I understand the full picture. The bug is that there's no `@ExternalLink` annotation that allows arbitrary external URLs (unlike `@KubeLink` which only generates Kubernetes API doc links). I need to:

1. Create the `@ExternalLink` annotation in `crd-generator/src/main/java/io/strimzi/crdgenerator/annotations/`
2. Update the `Linker` interface to support `ExternalLink`
3. Update the `KubeLinker` (or create a default implementation) to support `ExternalLink`
4. Update `DocGenerator` to handle `@ExternalLink`

Let me first add a test to reproduce the current state (no `@ExternalLink` support). Let me create a test that would fail without the fix.

But first, let me check the pom.xml for the crd-generator to understand the build setup:

<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"

    <parent>

    <licenses>
        <license>

    <properties>

    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.github.spotbugs</groupId>
            <artifactId>spotbugs-annotations</artifactId>
        </dependency>

            <artifactId>kubernetes-client-api</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>io.fabric8</groupId>
            <artifactId>kubernetes-model-core</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-annotations</artifactId>

            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.hamcrest</groupId>
            <artifactId>hamcrest</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>

                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>analyze</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>analyze-only</goal>

                        <configuration>
                            <failOnWarning>true</failOnWarning>

                            </ignoredUnusedDeclaredDependencies>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-shade-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${maven.shade.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <phase>package</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>shade</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <createDependencyReducedPom>false</createDependencyReducedPom>
                            <transformers>

                                        implementation="org.apache.maven.plugins.shade.resource.ServicesResourceTransformer"/>

                                        implementation="org.apache.maven.plugins.shade.resource.ManifestResourceTransformer">

                                </transformer>

                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>
</project>

The test is running but failing because the URL format in the generated docs doesn't precisely match my expected format. Let me check what the actual output looks like. The test's first assertion (line with `contains("https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/")`) seems to pass since it gets to line 35. The issue is my expected format for the link is wrong. Let me look at the `appendPropertyType` method to understand the exact format:

Looking at DocGenerator line 299, the format is:
```
externalUrl + "[" + propertyClass.getSimpleName() + "]"
```

So for a `String` property, the output would be:
`https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/reference/spec/#gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1.HTTPRoute[String]`

Not `[HTTPRoute]`. Let me update the test to check for the correct format:

content.text:
Here's a summary of all the changes I made to fix the bug:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
There was no `@ExternalLink` annotation that could be used as a generic alternative to `@KubeLink` for linking to external documentation URLs (e.g., Gateway API CRDs that are not part of the main Kubernetes API).

### Files Modified

1. **New file: `crd-generator/src/main/java/io/strimzi/crdgenerator/annotations/ExternalLink.java`**
   - Created a new annotation `@ExternalLink` that accepts a `url` parameter. It can be applied to methods and fields, similar to `@KubeLink`.

2. **Modified: `crd-generator/src/main/java/io/strimzi/crdgenerator/Linker.java`**
   - Added a new `link(ExternalLink externalLink)` method to the `Linker` interface to support generating URLs from `@ExternalLink` annotations.

3. **Modified: `crd-generator/src/main/java/io/strimzi/crdgenerator/KubeLinker.java`**
   - Implemented the new `link(ExternalLink externalLink)` method, which simply returns the URL from the annotation directly.

4. **Modified: `crd-generator/src/main/java/io/strimzi/crdgenerator/DocGenerator.java`**
   - Added import for `ExternalLink`
   - Updated `usedIn()` method to also skip properties annotated with `@ExternalLink` (same as `@KubeLink`), so the external link type is not treated as a nested type that needs its own documentation section.
   - Updated `appendCommonTypeDoc()` to check for `@ExternalLink` annotation on properties and generate external links when found, using the same mechanism as `@KubeLink`.

### How it works
Users can now annotate properties with `@ExternalLink(url = "https://...")` to link to any external documentation. The DocGenerator will render it as: `url[PropertyType]` in the generated AsciiDoc output, following the same pattern as `@KubeLink`.

Here's a summary of all the changes I made to fix the bug:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
There was no `@ExternalLink` annotation that could be used as a generic alternative to `@KubeLink` for linking to external documentation URLs (e.g., Gateway API CRDs that are not part of the main Kubernetes API).

### Files Modified

1. **New file: `crd-generator/src/main/java/io/strimzi/crdgenerator/annotations/ExternalLink.java`**
   - Created a new annotation `@ExternalLink` that accepts a `url` parameter. It can be applied to methods and fields, similar to `@KubeLink`.

2. **Modified: `crd-generator/src/main/java/io/strimzi/crdgenerator/Linker.java`**
   - Added a new `link(ExternalLink externalLink)` method to the `Linker` interface to support generating URLs from `@ExternalLink` annotations.

3. **Modified: `crd-generator/src/main/java/io/strimzi/crdgenerator/KubeLinker.java`**
   - Implemented the new `link(ExternalLink externalLink)` method, which simply returns the URL from the annotation directly.

4. **Modified: `crd-generator/src/main/java/io/strimzi/crdgenerator/DocGenerator.java`**
   - Added import for `ExternalLink`
   - Updated `usedIn()` method to also skip properties annotated with `@ExternalLink` (same as `@KubeLink`), so the external link type is not treated as a nested type that needs its own documentation section.
   - Updated `appendCommonTypeDoc()` to check for `@ExternalLink` annotation on properties and generate external links when found, using the same mechanism as `@KubeLink`.

### How it works
Users can now annotate properties with `@ExternalLink(url = "https://...")` to link to any external documentation. The DocGenerator will render it as: `url[PropertyType]` in the generated AsciiDoc output, following the same pattern as `@KubeLink`.
